narration_script: Two weeks into Operation Epic Fury, the mission to eliminate Iran's ballistic missiles and nuclear ambitions is triggering a severe global crisis. Military assets are dispersed across a vast landscape, often buried deep in mountain bunkers. The conflict escalated rapidly after the Supreme Leader was killed on the very first day. Violence has since spread to Lebanon and even a British airbase in Cyprus. The human cost is staggering, with over three million people now displaced by the fighting. Global markets are reeling as the vital Strait of Hormuz is effectively choked off. Governments worldwide are now scrambling to address surging energy costs and potential price gouging. This sudden escalation has left Washington’s Gulf allies in a state of profound shock. Despite long-term efforts to pivot toward Asia, the Middle East continues to dominate American foreign policy, as regional instability threatens to prolong the conflict.

key_points.label: Campaign Objectives
key_points.excerpt: A campaign to rid Iran of its ballistic missiles, navy and any lingering ambitions to develop a nuclear weapon
key_points.script_anchor: eliminate Iran's ballistic missiles and nuclear ambitions

key_points.label: Dispersed Military Assets
key_points.excerpt: Its military assets, such as parts of its nuclear programme, are dispersed across a huge country, sometimes buried deep in mountain bunkers.
key_points.script_anchor: dispersed across a vast landscape, often buried

key_points.label: Leadership Targeted
key_points.excerpt: Iran’s Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, and many key regime figures, were killed on day one.
key_points.script_anchor: Supreme Leader was killed on the very first day

key_points.label: Regional Conflict Spread
key_points.excerpt: Within days, the British RAF base at Akrotiri, Cyprus, had been hit.
key_points.script_anchor: even a British airbase in Cyprus

key_points.label: Mass Civilian Displacement
key_points.excerpt: The UN says as many as 3.2 million people have been displaced so far
key_points.script_anchor: over three million people now displaced by

key_points.label: Global Economic Impact
key_points.excerpt: The Strait of Hormuz, a narrow waterway through which about 20% of the world’s oil passes, has been all-but choked off.
key_points.script_anchor: Strait of Hormuz is effectively choked off

key_points.label: Energy Price Crisis
key_points.excerpt: In Britain and Europe, governments say they will crack down on price gouging
key_points.script_anchor: surging energy costs and potential price gouging

key_points.label: Shock to Gulf Allies
key_points.excerpt: For Washington’s Gulf allies, including Saudi Arabia and the UAE, the war has come as a profound shock.
key_points.script_anchor: Washington’s Gulf allies in a state of profound shock

key_points.label: US Foreign Policy Shift
key_points.excerpt: The war in Iran demonstrates, yet again, that the wider Middle East has a way of sucking America back in.
key_points.script_anchor: Middle East continues to dominate American foreign policy
